task_completion.description: Verify the creation of the 'critical-servers' agent group.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Wazuh 'Groups' management page showing 'critical-servers' in the list of available groups.

task_completion.description: Verify agent 000 is assigned to the 'critical-servers' group.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the 'critical-servers' group details showing agent 000 as a member, or the agent 000 details page showing 'critical-servers' under its assigned groups.

task_completion.description: Verify FIM (syscheck) is configured for the group with the required paths.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the group configuration editor (e.g., agent.conf for critical-servers) in the Wazuh UI containing a <syscheck> section with realtime monitoring enabled for paths including /etc/passwd, /etc/shadow, /etc/ssh, /etc/audit, and /var/log/auth (or auth.log).

task_completion.description: Verify a custom detection rule is created for the FIM events.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the custom rules editor (e.g., local_rules.xml) in the Wazuh UI showing a new rule with a severity level of 10 or higher that triggers on syscheck/FIM events.

task_completion.description: Verify an active response is configured to trigger on the custom rule.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Wazuh manager configuration (ossec.conf) in the UI showing an <active-response> block that references the newly created custom rule ID and specifies a host-based command/action.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Wazuh web dashboard to perform the configurations.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Wazuh web interface (https://localhost) being navigated, rather than just terminal windows echoing text into files.

integrity.description: Verify the configurations were actually saved and applied in the Wazuh interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for success toast notifications ('Configuration saved successfully', 'Rule saved', etc.) or active group/agent status in the dashboard, rather than just typed text in an unsaved text box.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not spoof the results using a text editor or fake HTML.
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser URL bar should show the correct Wazuh dashboard URLs, and the interface elements (menus, buttons, sidebars) should be consistent with the actual Wazuh application.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target group must be exactly 'critical-servers'. Target agent ID must be '000'. The configured FIM paths should include /etc/passwd, /etc/shadow, /etc/ssh, /etc/audit, and /var/log/auth (or auth.log). The custom rule level must be 10 or greater (the verifier accepts 10+, despite the prompt asking for 12 or higher).
